name: File + AI Action + Conditional + Alert
description: Plan-to-commands evaluation captured from production log on 2026-04-30 at 15:57:07.
user_prompt:
Convert this workflow plan to JSON commands:

1. File node — Read the configuration file from disk.
   - Node type: File
   - Operation: read
   - File path: C:\\config\\settings.json
   - saveToVariable: true
   - outputVariable: settingsJsonText
   - Purpose: Load the raw JSON content from C:\\config\\settings.json into a workflow variable for downstream processing.

2. AI Action node — Use AI agent ID 372 to extract the value of the JSON key error_threshold.
   - Node type: AI Action
   - Agent ID: 372
   - Input/prompt: Read the following JSON content and extract the value of the key named error_threshold. Return only the raw value with no explanation, no JSON wrapper, and no extra text.

JSON content:
${settingsJsonText}
   - Output variable: extractedErrorThresholdRaw
   - Purpose: Reliably isolate the error_threshold value from the JSON file content.

3. Set Variable node — Convert the extracted value into a numeric variable for comparison.
   - Node type: Set Variable
   - Variable name: error_threshold
   - Mode: Python expression
   - Expression: float(extractedErrorThresholdRaw)
   - Purpose: Normalize the AI output into a numeric variable that the Conditional node can compare.

4. Set Variable node — Prepare the alert email body in a simple standalone variable because Alert messageTemplate must reference only a simple variable name.
   - Node type: Set Variable
   - Variable name: alertMessage
   - Mode: Python expression
   - Expression: f"settings.json error_threshold is {error_threshold}, which exceeds the limit of 100. File checked: C:\\config\\settings.json"
   - Purpose: Build the email body in a separate variable for validator-safe use in the Alert node.

5. Set Variable node — Prepare the alert email subject in a simple standalone variable because Alert emailSubject must reference only a simple variable name.
   - Node type: Set Variable
   - Variable name: alertSubject
   - Mode: Python expression
   - Expression: f"High error_threshold detected: {error_threshold}"
   - Purpose: Build the email subject in a separate variable for validator-safe use in the Alert node.

6. Conditional node — Check whether error_threshold exceeds 100.
   - Node type: Conditional
   - conditionType: comparison
   - Left value: ${error_threshold}
   - Operator: greater than
   - Right value: 100
   - Purpose: Branch only when the threshold is above the specified limit.

7. Alert node — On the Conditional pass path, send an email alert to admin@acme.com.
   - Node type: Alert
   - Alert type: Email
   - To: admin@acme.com
   - emailSubject: ${alertSubject}
   - messageTemplate: ${alertMessage}
   - Purpose: Notify the administrator when error_threshold is greater than 100.

8. Connections
   - Connect File node pass output -> AI Action node
   - Connect AI Action node pass output -> Set Variable node (error_threshold)
   - Connect Set Variable node (error_threshold) pass output -> Set Variable node (alertMessage)
   - Connect Set Variable node (alertMessage) pass output -> Set Variable node (alertSubject)
   - Connect Set Variable node (alertSubject) pass output -> Conditional node
   - Connect Conditional node pass output -> Alert node
   - Leave the Conditional fail path with no further action (workflow ends with no alert when error_threshold is 100 or below).

9. Defaults and implementation notes
   - Build this as a manually run workflow.
   - Use agent ID 372 exactly as requested.
   - Assume the file contains valid JSON.
   - Reasonable default behavior: if the value is greater than 100, send one email; otherwise do nothing.
